name: The Undermall
short_description: This is an anomaly game set inside a mall. Several Jump scares. Fun little "Spot the difference game" with a touch of horror. Get it correct 10 times in a row and you win. You can consider this "Early Release" But the first store is done and if this does well I'll add more in the future :)
detailed_description:
Welcome to The Undermall! You are trapped in a mall where you must spot the differences to gain.... freedom? Escape? Be set free? 

In this game you find yourself in front of the "Warm Subject". A nice little goth store. When you enter, study it. See what all is there, and where it's at. When you think you have it down, go through the "Green Exit" to begin. You will find yourself right back in front of the store again. BUT! this time, when you enter, something MIGHT be different. It could be a small change such as missing jewelry. It could be glaringly obvious. It might just JUMP out and scare you. OR it could be nothing. If there is any change then you go through the anomaly door (the red exit). If nothing is changed then go through the safe door (the green exit). Be careful! If you get it wrong then you're back to room 0 (which will always be a safe room for you to re study). Get it right 10 times in a row and you win.  

NOTE: There will NEVER be audio only anomalies. 

Controls are simple. Standard WASD movement, Shift to sprint, Ctrl to crouch, Space to jump, and Right click (hold) to zoom in. I've added a fun little emote if you press the tab key. 

Low, Medium, High, and Ultra graphics settings. Mouse sensitivity slider. Music and SFX sliders. 

There is a simple "Character Customization" platform you can enter if you wish to change the color and type of clothing you're wearing. I plan on expanding customization more in the future.

There is also a "Speed Run Overlay" you can enable in the settings at any time. Let me see what you can do Speed Runners!

While there are no "Easy, Medium, or Hard" settings I would consider this a "Medium" difficulty game. None of the anomalies are so small as to illicit the "How would anyone EVER notice that" but do pay close attention. I can't make it so easy that it's just a HAND out.

There is no story mode or any dialog. It just is. I MAY add a one time cut scene in the future.
